expectations.text: build() returns render array with #cache key containing BOTH 'tags' AND 'contexts' (the golden rule: EVERY render array needs #cache)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 96-103 (empty case) and lines 126-140 (main return) both have '#cache' keys containing both 'tags' and 'contexts'. Line 98-100: '#cache' => ['tags' => ['node_list'], 'contexts' => ['route', 'user'], ...]. Lines 130-139: '#cache' => ['tags' => $tags, 'contexts' => ['route', 'user'], ...].

expectations.text: Cache tags include node-specific tags (node:ID pattern) for data dependency invalidation
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 112-115: foreach ($nodes as $node) { $tags = Cache::mergeTags($tags, $node->getCacheTags()); } -- $node->getCacheTags() returns ['node:ID'] for each loaded node. These tags are then used in the #cache array at line 133.

expectations.text: Cache contexts include 'route' or 'route.name' AND 'user' or 'user.permissions' or 'user.roles'
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 100: 'contexts' => ['route', 'user'] and line 136: 'contexts' => ['route', 'user']. Both return paths include 'route' AND 'user' cache contexts. Also getCacheContexts() at line 185 merges ['route', 'user'].

expectations.text: Does NOT set max-age to 0 anywhere (max-age: 0 kills the ENTIRE page cache via bubbling -- use lazy_builder instead)
expectations.evidence: grep for 'max-age.*0|max_age.*0' in the module directory returned only comment lines (line 137: '// Permanent: tag-based invalidation handles freshness -- never max-age 0.' and line 204: '// Never use max-age 0, ...'). Actual max-age values are Cache::PERMANENT (lines 101, 138, 205).

expectations.text: Does NOT use \Drupal::cache() for render caching (render arrays use #cache, not manual cache API)
expectations.evidence: grep for '\\Drupal::cache()' in the module directory returned 'No matches found'. The module uses #cache render array metadata exclusively.

expectations.text: If the block varies per user, uses cache context 'user' (NOT max-age: 0 as a workaround for per-user variation)
expectations.evidence: The block varies per user (line 171: accessCheck(TRUE) makes query results user-dependent). It uses 'user' cache context (lines 100, 136, 185) and Cache::PERMANENT for max-age (lines 101, 138, 205). No max-age: 0 is used anywhere.

expectations.text: #cache metadata is on the OUTERMOST render array element (not buried inside a child element where it won't bubble correctly)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 96-103: empty case returns top-level array with '#cache' alongside '#markup'. Lines 126-140: main return has '#cache' at the same level as '#theme', '#items', '#title' -- the outermost render array element. The #cache is NOT inside any child element in #items.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en related_content_block returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en related_content_block -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: related_content_block' with EXIT_CODE: 0. Module status confirmed as 'Enabled' via ddev drush pm:list.

expectations.text: E2E: Block renders without errors after placement
expectations.evidence:
After placing the block in the sidebar region and creating 3 test article nodes, curl -sk https://d10-caching-with.ddev.site/node/1 shows the block rendered: '

Related Content

Related Content

  • Test Article 2
  • Test Article 3
'. No PHP errors, warnings, or exceptions found in the output.
